active_goal: Improve MemoryOS Lite v3 into a benchmark-usable Letta-style agent memory system for LongMemEval and LoCoMo, without demo-only phase completion, without hiding case-level regressions, and without enabling the v3 kernel by default.

context_bundle.summary: Phase 10 must target real LoCoMo recall/session-localization failures from Phase 9, preserve v3 default/v1 fallback/kernel opt-in, require RED tests before code changes, and prove same-case movement through the v3/public benchmark path.

objective: Improve or precisely reclassify a repeated LoCoMo recall/session-localization failure class without regressing LongMemEval or changing kernel defaults.

red_evidence_required_before_implementat.description: A LoCoMo-like weak same-session anchor is lost to wrong-session lexical hits in RecallMemorySearcher or RecallPipeline.
Unrelated session neighbors are not pulled into evidence after the recall change.
A strong direct LongMemEval-like hit remains stable after recall/session expansion behavior changes.

milestone_eval_commands.longmemeval_30: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark longmemeval --data-path benchmarks/longmemeval/longmemeval.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 30 --llm-answer --llm-judge
milestone_eval_commands.locomo_30: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark locomo --data-path benchmarks/locomo/locomo10.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 30 --llm-answer --llm-judge

usable_ack_checklist: Every Phase 10 artifact has phase binding.
Plan, execute, and review artifacts cite work/phase-10/context_bundle.md.
At least one repeated LoCoMo retrieval/session class improves or is converted into a more precise downstream class.
LoCoMo 30 has same-case explainable signal, not aggregate-only movement.
Pass-to-fail, fail-to-pass, unchanged-fail, retrieval/source movement, and failure-class movement are listed separately.
No case-id hacks, expected-answer leaks, scoring changes, v1 fallback regression, v3 default regression, or kernel-default change.
Review lane verdict is PASS or usable_ack before ACK.
review_focus: Does the implementation exercise the real v3/public benchmark path?
Did tests fail before the recall behavior change?
Are LoCoMo session-localization misses actually improved or reclassified with evidence?
Are source/retrieval metrics kept separate from judged answer quality?
Were LongMemEval and LoCoMo 30 gates run in parallel when required?
Were heartbeat/partial monitor artifacts updated for long evals?
Are the pre-existing dirty AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and blueprint changes preserved?
